diagrams.title: Register Transfer Level (RTL) Arithmetic Microoperations
diagrams.description: Shows the data flow between registers during arithmetic microoperations using RTL notation. Displays how two source registers (R1, R2) transfer data to the ALU, perform the operation, and store the result in a destination register (R3). Includes control signals and the bus system.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how arithmetic microoperations are specified at the register transfer level and how data moves between registers during computation

diagrams.title: ALU Block Diagram for Arithmetic Operations
diagrams.description: Illustrates the internal structure of an ALU focusing on arithmetic microoperations. Shows the functional units: binary adder, subtractor, incrementer, decrementer, and logic gates. Includes selector lines (S0, S1, S2) to choose between operations.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the hardware implementation of arithmetic microoperations within the ALU and how the operation is selected

diagrams.title: Two's Complement Arithmetic: Addition and Overflow
diagrams.description: Visual step-by-step examples showing 4-bit two's complement addition and subtraction. Includes examples of no overflow and overflow conditions. Shows how the sign bit propagates and when the overflow flag is set.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how signed arithmetic works in computer systems and when overflow conditions occur during arithmetic microoperations
